nodes.label: Thermoplastic Polyurethane and Its Shape-Memory Behavior
nodes.full_title: Thermoplastic Polyurethane and Its Shape-Memory Behavior
nodes.authors: Not fully extracted (Monash University, Australia)
nodes.materials_raw: Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), shape memory polymers, soft segments (polyol), hard segments (diisocyanate + chain extender)
nodes.methods_raw: DSC, DMA, tensile testing, shape memory testing (programming/recovery)
nodes.key_findings: TPU shape memory behavior depends on phase separation between hard/soft segments; switching temperature relates to Tg or Tm of soft segment; shape fixity and recovery ratios characterize performance
nodes.scientific_questions: How to precisely control phase separation? What determines switching temperature accuracy?

nodes.label: High-barrier polyimide containing fluorenol moiety
nodes.full_title: High-barrier polyimide containing fluorenol moiety
nodes.authors: Yiwu Liu, Ao Tang, Jinghua Tan et al.
nodes.materials_raw: Polyimide (PI), 9-OH-FPPI (fluorenol-containing PI), PMDA, ODA, Kapton
nodes.methods_raw: Molecular simulation (COMPASS force field, Materials Studio), gas permeability (Mocon), WAXD, DMA, TGA, PALS (positron annihilation), density measurement
nodes.key_findings: Fluorenol moiety in PI backbone improves gas barrier properties; free volume analysis via molecular simulation correlates with experimental PALS data; 9-OH-FPPI shows lower gas permeability than Kapton
nodes.scientific_questions: How does fluorenol OH group affect chain packing? What is the relationship between free volume distribution and gas diffusion mechanism?

nodes.label: (Phase-pinned bicontinuous structures in ionic elastomers)
nodes.full_title: (Phase-pinned bicontinuous structures in ionic elastomers)
nodes.authors: Wang et al.
nodes.materials_raw: Ionic elastomers, bicontinuous structures, phase-pinned morphology
nodes.methods_raw: SAXS, rheology, mechanical testing
nodes.key_findings: Phase pinning strategy locks bicontinuous morphology; ionic interactions provide physical crosslinks; combined high strength and elasticity
nodes.scientific_questions: How to balance phase separation and ionic crosslinking? What controls the domain size?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Complex Architectures
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Complex Architectures
nodes.authors: Polymeropoulos et al.
nodes.materials_raw: Star, miktoarm, cyclic, comb, graft polymers; block copolymers
nodes.methods_raw: Living polymerization (anionic, ATRP, RAFT), SEC, self-assembly characterization
nodes.key_findings: Complex polymer architectures (star, miktoarm, cyclic, comb, graft) provide unique self-assembly and property profiles; advances in living polymerization enable precise architecture control
nodes.scientific_questions: How does architecture affect phase behavior? Can we make arbitrarily complex architectures?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Phase Behavior
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Phase Behavior
nodes.authors: Knychala et al.
nodes.materials_raw: Block copolymers, Flory-Huggins interaction parameter χ
nodes.methods_raw: Self-consistent field theory (SCFT), SAXS, phase diagram construction
nodes.key_findings: Flory-Huggins theory and SCFT predict BCP phase behavior; χ parameter controls morphology; experimental validation through systematic studies
nodes.scientific_questions: How to accurately measure χ? Predicting phase behavior for complex architectures?

nodes.label: Supertough TPU with H-bonding arrays
nodes.full_title: Supertough TPU with H-bonding arrays
nodes.authors: Tian et al.
nodes.institution: Qingdao University of Science and Technology
nodes.materials_raw: TPU with ASCZ (ureidopyrimidinone) H-bonding arrays, supramolecular crosslinks
nodes.methods_raw: Mechanical testing (tensile, cyclic), SAXS, DSC, FT-IR, DMA
nodes.key_findings: H-bonding arrays (UPy groups) create strong but reversible physical crosslinks; achieves unprecedented toughness-resilience combination; hierarchical phase separation with H-bonding domains
nodes.scientific_questions: How to optimize H-bonding density for maximum toughness? Temperature dependence of H-bonding reversibility?

nodes.label: Qingdao University of Science and Technology
50th Anniversary Perspective: Functional Nanoparticles from Solution Self-Assembly of Block Copolyme
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Functional Nanoparticles from Solution Self-Assembly of Block Copolymers
nodes.authors: Tritschler, Pearce, Gwyther, Whittell, Manners
nodes.materials_raw: BCPs (PS-b-PI, PS-b-PAA, PFDMS-b-PDMS, PEO-b-PS, PLLA-b-PAA), crystalline-core micelles
nodes.methods_raw: TEM, DLS, SAXS, cryo-TEM, self-seeding, seeded growth (CDSA)
nodes.key_findings: Packing parameter P predicts equilibrium morphology (spheres, cylinders, vesicles); CDSA enables monodisperse cylindrical micelles via living crystallization-driven self-assembly; multicompartment nanoparticles from triblock terpolymers; cross-linking locks micelle structure
nodes.scientific_questions: How to achieve kinetic vs thermodynamic control? Size uniformity in CDSA?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Putting the Squeeze on Polymers
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Putting the Squeeze on Polymers
nodes.authors: Thomas P. Russell, Yu Chai
nodes.materials_raw: PS thin films, PMMA, block copolymer thin films, conjugated polyelectrolytes (CPEs), conjugated polymer zwitterions (CPZs)
nodes.methods_raw: Ellipsometry, neutron reflectivity, SANS, AFM, XPCS, DSC
nodes.key_findings: Tg reduction in thin films due to mobile surface layer (~2.3 nm); chain dimensions in thin films debated (SANS contradictory); directed self-assembly of BCPs for nanopatterning; random copolymer brushes control interfacial interactions; CPEs/CPZs modify electrode work function in OPVs
nodes.scientific_questions: Does Tg truly reduce at free surfaces? How far do surface effects propagate? Perfect lateral ordering of BCP domains?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: There Is a Great Future in Sustainable Polymers
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: There Is a Great Future in Sustainable Polymers
nodes.authors: Deborah K. Schneiderman, Marc A. Hillmyer
nodes.materials_raw: Bio-derived polymers (PLA, PHA, isosorbide derivatives), degradable polymers, reprocessable thermosets
nodes.methods_raw: Ring-opening polymerization, polycondensation, LCA, catalytic degradation
nodes.key_findings: 5 key areas: renewable monomers, degradable polymers, chemical recycling, reprocessable thermosets, advanced catalysis; bio-derived ≠ always more sustainable; LCA complexity; isohexide-based polyesters from sugars
nodes.scientific_questions: Cost-competitiveness of bio-derived monomers? Designing polymers with controlled degradation rates?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Brushes: Novel Surfaces for Future Materials
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Brushes: Novel Surfaces for Future Materials
nodes.authors: Wei-Liang Chen, Roselynn Cordero, Hai Tran, Christopher K. Ober
nodes.materials_raw: Polymer brushes (PMMA, PS, PBA, PSPMA), initiator-modified substrates
nodes.methods_raw: SI-ATRP, SI-RAFT, SI-NMP, SI-PIMP, light-mediated CRP, ellipsometry, neutron reflectivity, QCM-D
nodes.key_findings: Alexander-de Gennes brush theory: h ~ N(σw)^{1/3}; charged brushes show different scaling; light-mediated CRP enables 3D patterning; Σ > 5 defines brush state; brushes resist nonspecific protein binding
nodes.scientific_questions: Precise control of brush density? Brush behavior on curved surfaces?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Crystals and Crystallization
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Crystals and Crystallization
nodes.authors: Bernard Lotz, Toshikazu Miyoshi, Stephen Z.D. Cheng
nodes.materials_raw: iPP (α, β, γ phases), PLLA, PE, st-PP, iPBu, i-P2VP
nodes.methods_raw: WAXD, electron diffraction, synchrotron X-ray, epitaxial crystallization, dark field imaging
nodes.key_findings: γ-iPP phase has nonparallel chain axes (~80°); frustrated polymer crystal structures widespread; chain folding impacts unit cell symmetry; structural disorder (up-down, lateral stacking) common; single crystal habits determined by slowest-growing plane
nodes.scientific_questions: Chain folding mechanism still debated? Extent of frustration in polymer crystals?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Functionalization
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Polymer Functionalization
nodes.authors: Eva Blasco, Michael B. Sims, Anja S. Goldmann, Brent S. Sumerlin, Christopher Barner-Kowollik
nodes.materials_raw: Poly(NHS acrylate), poly(PFP acrylate), activated esters, squaric esters
nodes.methods_raw: Post-polymerization modification, CuAAC, thiol-ene, NHS-ester amidation, PFP transesterification
nodes.key_findings: NHS and PFP esters are versatile activated esters for polymer modification; PFP esters superior solubility and orthogonality; click chemistry criteria for useful modifications; RDRP + activated esters = powerful combination; squaric esters show unusual hydrolytic stability
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving quantitative modification without side reactions? New activated ester chemistries?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Conducting/Semiconducting Conjugated Polymers
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Conducting/Semiconducting Conjugated Polymers
nodes.authors: Timothy M. Swager
nodes.materials_raw: Polyacetylene, polyaniline, PPV, poly(isothianaphthalene), poly(thienopyrazine), (SN)x
nodes.methods_raw: Electrical conductivity, band gap engineering, resonance structure analysis, doping
nodes.key_findings: (SN)x is only polymer superconductor (Tc=0.3 K); Peierls distortion explains semiconducting ground state; donor-acceptor design lowers band gap; competing aromatic resonance structures reduce gap; interchain π-stacking critical for conductivity; polyacene remains holy grail for intrinsic metallic polymer
nodes.scientific_questions: Can we make an intrinsically metallic organic polymer? Optimal D-A pairing for band gap control?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Advanced Polymer Fibers
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Advanced Polymer Fibers
nodes.authors: Jay Hoon Park, Gregory C. Rutledge
nodes.materials_raw: Carbon fibers (PAN, pitch, rayon precursors), Kevlar (PPTA), Nomex (MPIA), PBO (Zylon), PIPD (M5), UHMWPE (Spectra/Dyneema), Vectran
nodes.methods_raw: Dry-jet wet spinning, gel spinning, electrospinning, melt spinning, carbonization
nodes.key_findings: High performance fibers: 5-10x stiffer than steel per weight; Kevlar uses lyotropic liquid crystallinity for alignment; gel-spun UHMWPE achieves E=100-130 GPa; fiber moduli still 40-50% of theoretical; electrospinning creates submicron fibers with emergent properties
nodes.scientific_questions: Closing gap between measured and theoretical fiber modulus? Environmentally benign processing solvents?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Challenges in the Dynamics and Kinetics of Glass-Forming Polymers
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Challenges in the Dynamics and Kinetics of Glass-Forming Polymers
nodes.authors: Gregory B. McKenna, Sindee L. Simon
nodes.materials_raw: Polystyrene, poly(vinyl acetate), polybutadiene, atactic polypropylene
nodes.methods_raw: DSC, dilatometry, dielectric spectroscopy, viscoelastic spectroscopy, creep compliance
nodes.key_findings: Glass transition is "most interesting unsolved problem"; VFT/WLF describes super-Arrhenius behavior; Kovacs three signatures of structural recovery; time-temperature superposition breakdown well-known; Kauzmann paradox unresolved; 20 Ma amber tests for equilibrium below Tg; ultrastable glasses represent unexplored regime
nodes.scientific_questions: Does VFT continue below Tg? Resolution of Kauzmann paradox? Structure-property relationship in ultrastable glasses?

nodes.label: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Living Polymerization Emphasizing the Molecule in Macromolecules
nodes.full_title: 50th Anniversary Perspective: Living Polymerization Emphasizing the Molecule in Macromolecules
nodes.authors: Robert B. Grubbs, Robert H. Grubbs
nodes.materials_raw: Polystyrene, polybutadiene, various monomers for living polymerization
nodes.methods_raw: Living anionic polymerization, cationic polymerization, ROMP, ATRP, RAFT, NMP, SEC
nodes.key_findings: Szwarc's 1956 discovery of living anionic polymerization defined the field; SEC invention (1963) accelerated development; living polymerization enables polymers as organic molecules; RDRP methods (ATRP, RAFT, NMP) made living-like behavior accessible; concept extended from anionic to cationic, coordination, radical
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving true living character in radical polymerization? Sequence control through living methods?

nodes.label: Solid-State Multistimuli, Multiresponsive Polymeric Materials
nodes.full_title: Solid-State Multistimuli, Multiresponsive Polymeric Materials
nodes.authors: Katie M. Herbert, Stephen Schrettl, Stuart J. Rowan, Christoph Weder
nodes.materials_raw: Stimuli-responsive polymers, shape memory polymers, self-healing polymers, mechanochromic polymers (spiropyran), photonic crystals, liquid crystal elastomers
nodes.methods_raw: Shape memory programming/recovery, scratch healing tests, fluorescence spectroscopy, photonic crystal characterization
nodes.key_findings: MSMR (multistimuli, multiresponsive) polymers can respond to multiple stimuli with distinct responses; 4 healing mechanisms: polymer flow, dynamic bonds, reactive species, monomer capsules; shape memory uses Tg or Tm as fixing element; spiropyran mechanochromic; aggregachromic dyes; photonic crystals for color change
nodes.scientific_questions: How to achieve orthogonal responses to different stimuli? Mass transport limitations in solid-state chemoresponsive materials?

nodes.label: Polymers and Mixed Matrix Membranes for Gas and Vapor Separation
nodes.full_title: Polymers and Mixed Matrix Membranes for Gas and Vapor Separation
nodes.authors: Michele Galizia, Won Seok Chi, Zachary P. Smith, Timothy C. Merkel, Richard W. Baker, Benny D. Freeman
nodes.materials_raw: Polysulfone, polyimides, cellulose acetate, PTMSP, PPO, mixed matrix membranes (MMMs), MOFs, carbon molecular sieves
nodes.methods_raw: Gas permeability testing (constant volume/variable pressure), pure gas and mixed gas testing, Robeson upper bound analysis
nodes.key_findings: Solution-diffusion model: P = D × S; Robeson upper bound (1991, 2008); plasticization from CO2 causes mixed-gas selectivity loss; physical aging reduces permeability in thin films (<1 μm) by up to 70% over 1000h; 4 big commercial applications: H2 recovery, N2 production, natural gas treatment, vapor recovery (~$1.5B/year market); cross-linking controls plasticization; MMMs with MOFs show promise
nodes.scientific_questions: Can MMMs consistently exceed upper bound? How to prevent physical aging in ultrathin films?

nodes.label: pure gas and mixed gas testing
RAFT Polymerization: A User Guide
nodes.full_title: RAFT Polymerization: A User Guide
nodes.authors: Sébastien Perrier
nodes.materials_raw: RAFT agents (dithioesters, trithiocarbonates, xanthates, dithiocarbamates), MAMs (acrylates, acrylamides, styrenes), LAMs (VAc, NVP, NVC)
nodes.methods_raw: RAFT polymerization, SEC, block copolymer synthesis by sequential addition
nodes.key_findings: RAFT is degenerative transfer (unlike ATRP/NMP which use persistent radical effect); dead chains = number of radicals from initiator; livingness L = [CTA]0/([CTA]0 + 2f[I]0(1-e^{-kdt})); Z-group controls C=S reactivity: trithiocarbonates/dithiobenzoates for MAMs, xanthates/dithiocarbamates for LAMs; switchable RAFT agents (pH-responsive dithiocarbamate); >8000 publications; CSIRO seminal paper most cited in Macromolecules (>3300 citations)
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving sequence control in RAFT? Universal RAFT agents for both MAMs and LAMs?

nodes.label: block copolymer synthesis by sequential addition
Networks and Gels: Soft but Dynamic and Tough
nodes.full_title: Networks and Gels: Soft but Dynamic and Tough
nodes.authors: Costantino Creton
nodes.materials_raw: Elastomers (natural rubber, SBR, silicone), hydrogels, interpenetrating networks, dynamic bond networks
nodes.methods_raw: Fracture mechanics (Γ measurement), uniaxial tension, Gent model, Lake-Thomas theory, slip-link model (Rubinstein-Panyukov)
nodes.key_findings: Affine network model: μx = νxkBT; Gent model for large strain with finite extensibility parameter Jm; Lake-Thomas theory: Γ0 ~ UbνxNx^{3/2} ~ μx^{-1/2} (trade-off between stiffness and toughness); fracture energy Γ = Γ0(1+φ(aTv)) - velocity dependent; two dissipation mechanisms: covalent bond breakage (threshold) and molecular friction (velocity-dependent); IPN strategy for combining stiffness and toughness with strain-dependent dissipation
nodes.scientific_questions: How to break the stiffness-toughness trade-off? Understanding fracture in heterogeneous networks?

nodes.label: Polymer Conformation: A Pedagogical Review
nodes.full_title: Polymer Conformation: A Pedagogical Review
nodes.authors: Zhen-Gang Wang
nodes.materials_raw: Ideal chains (FJC, Gaussian, WLC), real chains with excluded volume, ring polymers
nodes.methods_raw: Statistical mechanics, path integral representation, Flory-Huggins theory, scaling analysis
nodes.key_findings: Ideal chain R_ee = N^{1/2}b_0; Kuhn length b = C_∞ b_0; Gaussian distribution valid for |R| << Nb_0; ring closure probability (Jacobson-Stockmayer factor) ~ N^{-3/2}; WLC model with persistence length l_p; Flory-Huggins χ parameter from pseudopotential; mean-field theory applicable because each chain interacts with O(N^{1/2}) other chains
nodes.scientific_questions: Non-Gaussian chain behavior in melts? Topological effects in ring polymers?

nodes.label: Block Polymers: Pure Potential
nodes.full_title: Block Polymers: Pure Potential
nodes.authors: Christopher M. Bates, Frank S. Bates
nodes.materials_raw: Block copolymers (PS-b-PI, PS-b-PDMS, PEO-b-PPO), bottlebrush block polymers, protein block polymers
nodes.methods_raw: Self-consistent field theory (SCFT), SAXS/SANS, TEM, DSC, living anionic polymerization, RAFT, ATRP
nodes.key_findings: >5000 papers in Macromolecules on block polymers; Leibler RPA (1980), Fredrickson-Helfand fluctuations (1987), Semenov strong segregation (1985); gyroid phase identified ~2 decades after star block polymers; monodisperse synthetic polymers (Đ=1.00001) via iterative coupling; dispersity as design tool; bottlebrush architectures
nodes.scientific_questions: Predicting morphology for complex architectures? Processing to retain nanostructure?

nodes.label: Polymeric Biomaterials: Diverse Functions Enabled by Advances in Macromolecular Chemistry
nodes.full_title: Polymeric Biomaterials: Diverse Functions Enabled by Advances in Macromolecular Chemistry
nodes.authors: Yingkai Liang, Linqing Li, Rebecca A. Scott, Kristi L. Kiick
nodes.materials_raw: PHEMA, PLGA, PVA, PEG, ELPs (elastin-like polypeptides), RLPs (resilin-like polypeptides), polypeptides from NCA polymerization
nodes.methods_raw: NCA ring-opening polymerization, ROMP, ATRP, RAFT, CuAAC, SPAAC, tetrazine ligation, thiol-ene, recombinant DNA technology
nodes.key_findings: Orthogonal click chemistries (CuAAC, SPAAC, tetrazine ligation, thiol-ene) enable biomaterial synthesis under physiological conditions; ELPs have inverse transition behavior (LCST); resilin-like polypeptides (RLPs) show exceptional resilience; recombinant methods give monodisperse sequence-controlled protein polymers; photomediated CuAAC and thiol-Michael for spatiotemporal control
nodes.scientific_questions: Scalable production of recombinant protein polymers? Long-term in vivo stability?

nodes.label: A Perspective on Polyelectrolyte Solutions
nodes.full_title: A Perspective on Polyelectrolyte Solutions
nodes.authors: M. Muthukumar
nodes.materials_raw: Polyelectrolytes (RNA, DNA, sodium poly(styrenesulfonate)), counterions, salt ions
nodes.methods_raw: Debye-Hückel theory, Bjerrum length analysis, Katchalsky free energy model, scaling theory
nodes.key_findings: Bjerrum length l_B = e²/(4πε₀εk_BT) ≈ 0.7nm in water at 25°C; Debye length ξ_D = 0.3/√c_s nm; salt-free polyelectrolyte R ~ N (rod-like); high salt R_g ~ N^{3/5} (self-avoiding walk); dipole-dipole interactions (υ_dipole ≈ -10k_BT) cause chain collapse; counterion adsorption creates temporary dipoles
nodes.scientific_questions: Theory of polyelectrolyte phase behavior? Dynamics of counterion release?

nodes.label: Celebrating 50 Years of Macromolecules
nodes.full_title: Celebrating 50 Years of Macromolecules
nodes.authors: Timothy P. Lodge
nodes.materials_raw: N/A (editorial)
nodes.methods_raw: N/A
nodes.key_findings: Top 10 achievements: (1) controlled radical polymerization, (2) block copolymer phase diagram, (3) polymer brush, (4) conducting polymers, (5) controlled nonlinear architectures, (6) reptation model, (7) biomedical polymers, (8) metallocene/ROMP catalysis, (9) RPA for blends, (10) dynamic/supramolecular polymers. Top 10 challenges: theory of crystallization, theory of glass transition, sustainable commodity polymers, monomer sequence control, better membranes, competitive sustainable polymers, personalized medicine, ion-containing polymers, robust plastic electronics, scale-up retaining nanostructure
nodes.scientific_questions: All challenges remain open

nodes.label: Editorial for Virtual Issue "50th Anniversary Perspectives"
nodes.full_title: Editorial for Virtual Issue "50th Anniversary Perspectives"
nodes.authors: Marc A. Hillmyer
nodes.materials_raw: N/A (editorial)
nodes.methods_raw: N/A
nodes.key_findings: 24 Perspectives published in 2017; >42000 publications in Macromolecules history; >100000 total citations per year; most cited journal in polymer science
nodes.scientific_questions: N/A

nodes.label: Are Polymer Nanocomposites Practical for Applications?
nodes.full_title: Are Polymer Nanocomposites Practical for Applications?
nodes.authors: Sanat K. Kumar, Brian C. Benicewicz, Richard A. Vaia, Karen I. Winey
nodes.materials_raw: Polymer nanocomposites (PNCs): silica, titania, C60, CNTs, clay, graphene, metal oxide NPs in various polymer matrices
nodes.methods_raw: Grafting-to, grafting-from surface functionalization, ATRP/RAFT/NMP from NP surfaces, SAXS/SANS for dispersion characterization, TEM
nodes.key_findings: PNCs are billion-dollar industry; 4 case studies: filled rubbers (successful), fiber-reinforced composites (niche), gas separation membranes (potential), dielectrics (limited); NP dispersion critical but not always maximum dispersion optimal; bimodal grafting (short dense + long sparse) improves dispersion; bound rubber layer important for reinforcement; hierarchical NP structures (strings, sheets, cellular) can optimize properties
nodes.scientific_questions: Best NP dispersion state for mechanical reinforcement? Scaling lab findings to industrial processing?

nodes.label: metal oxide NPs in various polymer matrices
Aromatic Heterocyclic Polymer Materials: Structure and Molecular Design
nodes.full_title: Aromatic Heterocyclic Polymer Materials: Structure and Molecular Design
nodes.authors: (Japanese authors, Toyo Boseki/Toyobo)
nodes.materials_raw: PPTA (Kevlar), PBO (Zylon), PIPD (M5), PBZT, co-polyaramid, polyimide
nodes.methods_raw: Lattice dynamics, molecular mechanics, X-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, crystal elastic modulus calculation
nodes.key_findings: PPTA crystal elastic modulus 182 GPa (calc) vs 188 GPa (expt); PBO fiber (Zylon) commercialized 1994 by Toyobo; PIPD has 3D hydrogen-bonded crystal structure via water molecules; co-polyaramid (M50) elastic modulus 106 GPa; negative linear expansion coefficient from thermal vibration anisotropy; lattice dynamics enables simultaneous calculation of vibrational frequencies and elastic constants
nodes.scientific_questions: Predicting crystal structure from molecular design? Improving compressive strength of PBO?

nodes.label: A Theory of the Linear Viscoelastic Properties of Dilute Solutions of Coiling Polymers
nodes.full_title: A Theory of the Linear Viscoelastic Properties of Dilute Solutions of Coiling Polymers
nodes.authors: Prince E. Rouse, Jr.
nodes.materials_raw: Coiling polymers in dilute solution (polystyrene, polyisobutylene)
nodes.methods_raw: Submolecule concept, orthogonal transformation, normal mode analysis
nodes.key_findings: Rouse model: polymer divided into N submolecules with Gaussian end-to-end distribution; velocity gradient perturbs configuration distribution; coordination resolved into series of modes each with characteristic relaxation time; complex viscosity η* = η₁ - iη₂; theory valid for dilute solutions where intermolecular contacts are infrequent; relaxation times increase rapidly with molecular weight
nodes.scientific_questions: Extending to concentrated solutions? Effect of entanglements?

nodes.label: Reversible Thiyl Radical Addition-Fragmentation Chain Transfer Polymerization
nodes.full_title: Reversible Thiyl Radical Addition-Fragmentation Chain Transfer Polymerization
nodes.authors: Yongjin Wang, Jiaman Du, Hanchu Huang
nodes.materials_raw: Cyclic allylic sulfides, allyl sulfide CTAs, thiyl radicals
nodes.methods_raw: SRAFT polymerization, SEC, NMR, MALDI-TOF, DFT calculations, chain extension
nodes.key_findings: First SRAFT polymerization strategy; allyl sulfides as CTAs for thiyl radical control; CTA3 (phenyl-substituted, dodecanyl R-group) optimal with Đ=1.29; linear Mn vs conversion; first-order kinetics; chain extension to diblock and triblock copolymers; DFT confirms reversible deactivation mechanism
nodes.scientific_questions: Expanding to other thiyl radical polymerizations? Sequence control?

nodes.label: Sticky Rouse Model and Molecular Simulation for Linear Viscoelasticity of Associative Polymers
nodes.full_title: Sticky Rouse Model and Molecular Simulation for Linear Viscoelasticity of Associative Polymers
nodes.authors: Zhang Lu, Jiang Nuofei, Shao Jingyu, Cui Xiang, Zhang Hongdong, Yang Yuliang, Tang Ping
nodes.materials_raw: Associative polymers (APs), ionomers, HEUR, hydrogen-bonding polymers
nodes.methods_raw: Sticky Rouse model (SRM), molecular dynamics simulation, graph theory for eigenvalue analysis
nodes.key_findings: SRM extends Rouse model with effective friction coefficient for sticker beads; relaxation spectrum splits into fast (strand) and slow (sticker) modes; bridge structures most important in APs; graph theory simplifies RZ matrix diagonalization; mean-field assumption valid when sticker lifetime >> strand relaxation time; τ_s ≈ τ_s0 exp(ΔE/k_BT)
nodes.scientific_questions: Nonlinear rheology of APs? SRM for entangled associative polymers?

nodes.label: Sticky Rouse Model and Molecular Dynamics Simulation for Dual Polymer Networks
nodes.full_title: Sticky Rouse Model and Molecular Dynamics Simulation for Dual Polymer Networks
nodes.authors: Jingyu Shao, Nuofei Jiang, Hongdong Zhang, Yuliang Yang, Ping Tang
nodes.materials_raw: Dual polymer networks (permanent + transient), phantom networks, dendrimer models
nodes.methods_raw: Modified SRM, graph theory for eigenvalues, MD simulation (Kremer-Grest bead-spring), FENE potential
nodes.key_findings: SRM combined with affine/phantom network models for dual networks; G(t) ∝ t^{-1/2} in sticker relaxation (Rouse-like); effective friction coefficient extracted from sticker lifetime and chain diffusion via MD; analytical eigenvalues via graph theory; parameter-free prediction of linear viscoelasticity
nodes.scientific_questions: Nonlinear mechanics of dual networks? Optimal sticker density for toughness?

nodes.label: Preparation of a Tough Elastomer by the Introduction of Multiple Flexible Hydrogen Bonds via Simple
nodes.full_title: Preparation of a Tough Elastomer by the Introduction of Multiple Flexible Hydrogen Bonds via Simple Modification
nodes.authors: Dian Zhang, Shintaro Nakagawa, Hirohiko Houjou, Naoko Yoshie
nodes.materials_raw: Epoxidized polybutadiene (PB-E), VAA (vinyl amine alcohol) modified PB, SBS block copolymer
nodes.methods_raw: Epoxidation with mCPBA, ring-opening with amines (ethylamine, butylamine, diethylamine), DFT calculations (B3LYP-GD3BJ), SEC, DSC
nodes.key_findings: VAA modification introduces multiple flexible H-bonds; DFT shows O-H is better H-bond donor than N-H; low rotational barriers confirm VAA flexibility; coarse-graining analysis for dimer vibrations; elastic constants estimated from H-bond dimer normal modes
nodes.scientific_questions: Optimal H-bond density for toughness? Temperature dependence of H-bond dynamics?

nodes.label: Rouse Mode Analysis of Chain Relaxation in Reversibly Crosslinked Polymer Melts
nodes.full_title: Rouse Mode Analysis of Chain Relaxation in Reversibly Crosslinked Polymer Melts
nodes.authors: Rahul Karmakar, Srikanth Sastry, Sanat K. Kumar, Tarak K. Patra
nodes.materials_raw: Kremer-Grest bead-spring polymer melt with reversible crosslinks (dissociative bond exchange)
nodes.methods_raw: Hybrid MD-Monte Carlo simulation, Rouse mode analysis, FENE bonds, LJ potential
nodes.key_findings: Bond lifetime follows exponential distribution (τ_b ≈ 18.5τ_c); for short bond lifetimes, Rouse-like dynamics with increased local friction; for long bond lifetimes, two distinct relaxation modes (fast Rouse + slow sticker); sticker-to-sticker distance shorter than expected from finite chain effects; results consistent with sticky Rouse model predictions
nodes.scientific_questions: Entangled reversibly crosslinked melts? Nonlinear response under flow?

nodes.label: Kremer-Grest bead-spring polymer melt with reversible crossl
Recycling of Polyurethanes: Where We Are and Where We Are Going
nodes.full_title: Recycling of Polyurethanes: Where We Are and Where We Are Going
nodes.authors: Gabriele Rossignolo, Giulio Malucelli, Alessandra Lorenzetti
nodes.materials_raw: Thermoplastic polyurethanes (TPU), thermosetting PU foams, elastomers
nodes.methods_raw: Mechanical recycling (extrusion, compression, injection moulding), glycolysis, hydrolysis, physical recycling (SRIM), biodegradation
nodes.key_findings: PU = 7.8% of EU plastics (6th place); flexible foams 36%, rigid foams 32%; allophanate dissociation ~100-120°C, biuret ~115-125°C, aromatic urethane ~180°C, isocyanurate ~300°C; glycolysis most important feedstock recycling; mechanical recycling limited to 2-3 cycles; biodegradation emerging; bio-sourced polyols reduce environmental impact
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving closed-loop PU recycling? Enzymatic degradation of crosslinked PU?

nodes.label: Nonlinear Shear Rheology of Concentrated PVA/Borax Aqueous Solution: Nonlinearity of Sticky Rouse Re
nodes.full_title: Nonlinear Shear Rheology of Concentrated PVA/Borax Aqueous Solution: Nonlinearity of Sticky Rouse Relaxation
nodes.authors: Yanjie Zhang, Quan Chen, Hiroshi Watanabe
nodes.materials_raw: PVA (Mw=101 kDa)/borax aqueous solution (w_PVA=15%, C_borax=15 mM)
nodes.methods_raw: Oscillatory rheology, start-up flow, flow cessation, step strain, rheo-dielectric measurements
nodes.key_findings: Neat PVA: concentrated but unentangled (J_e ≈ Rouse prediction); PVA/borax: percolated transient network with 〈τ〉=30s (vs 5.5ms neat); time-flow rate separable η⁻(t,γ̇); time-strain separable G(t,γ); conductivity unchanged under flow → network disruption followed by rapid reformation via intrinsic Rouse motion; conformational averaging mechanism for nonlinearity of sticky Rouse relaxation
nodes.scientific_questions: Quantitative prediction of nonlinear rheology? Role of FENE effects?

nodes.label: Synthesis of a Photo-Crosslinkable Elastomer for Stretchable Electronics and Its Use in Strain-Insen
nodes.full_title: Synthesis of a Photo-Crosslinkable Elastomer for Stretchable Electronics and Its Use in Strain-Insensitive Pressure Sensors
nodes.authors: Hyon-Gyu Park, Minsung Kim, Hayeong Park, Joon Hak Oh
nodes.materials_raw: Butyl acrylate (BA), 2-hydroxyethyl acrylate (HEA), 2-isocyanatoethyl acrylate (IEA), DPHA (dipentaerythritol penta/hexa acrylate), SEBS, CNT electrodes, spiropyran (visualization dye)
nodes.methods_raw: UV photomask patterning, electrospinning, capacitance measurement, mechanical cycling (700 cycles at 100% strain)
nodes.key_findings: UV crosslinking through photomask creates rigid/soft island pattern; modulus increase up to 38000% (0.12→46.7 MPa with DPHA); soft regions retain low modulus; rigid islands (100 μm diameter, 50 μm spacing) maintain shape under 100% strain; island spacing increases ~300% while island dimensions unchanged; strain-insensitive pressure sensor (SIPS) using rigid islands as capacitor dielectric + electrospun layer for 780× pressure sensitivity amplification
nodes.scientific_questions: How does modulus gradient at pattern boundary affect stress concentration? Long-term fatigue behavior of rigid/soft interface? Scalability to large-area devices?

nodes.label: Robust and Fast-Transforming Soft Microrobots Driven by Low Magnetic Field
nodes.full_title: Robust and Fast-Transforming Soft Microrobots Driven by Low Magnetic Field
nodes.authors: Yuanyuan Wang, Haili Qin, Niu Liu, Qin-Nan Hu, Huai-Ping Cong, Shu-Hong Yu
nodes.materials_raw: Single-domain NdFeB nanoparticles (200-300 nm), polyacrylamide (PAM) grafted chains, gold nanoparticles (5-10 nm), acrylamide monomer
nodes.methods_raw: Ball milling for SD/MD particle preparation, magnetic hysteresis loops, SAED TEM, UV-initiated "grafting from" polymerization, magnetic field-assisted 3D printing (DIW), dynamic rheology
nodes.key_findings: SD-NdFeB (200-300 nm) has coercivity 678 mT vs MD 139 mT; saturation magnetization 125.85 vs 58.81 Am²/kg; hysteresis loop area 10× larger for SD; SD particles show direct moment rotation without domain wall motion; PAM grafting enables H-bond crosslinked gel ink; ultrafine filaments (80 μm) achieved; <2 wt% NdFeB sufficient; shape morphing in <1 s at 3-15 mT; 1600% stretchability; 10× mechanical toughness improvement from magnetic alignment; self-healing and reprogramming capability
nodes.scientific_questions: How to optimize SD particle size distribution for maximum coercivity? What is the minimum NdFeB loading for reliable actuation? Biocompatibility of Au@PAM shell in vivo?

nodes.label: Ball milling for SD/MD particle preparation
Mechanically Robust, Readily Repairable Polymers via Tailored Noncovalent Cross-linking
nodes.full_title: Mechanically Robust, Readily Repairable Polymers via Tailored Noncovalent Cross-linking
nodes.authors: Yu Yanagisawa, Yiling Nan, Kou Okuro, Takuzo Aida
nodes.materials_raw: Poly(ether-thiourea) TUEG₃ (Mn=9500, Mw=22300), TUEG₂, TUEG₄, poly(alkylene-thiourea) TUC₈, TUC₁₂, poly(ether-urea) UEG₃ (reference)
nodes.methods_raw: DSC, FTIR, XRD, DMA (temperature/frequency sweep), rheometer-based healing test (Corté method), time-temperature superposition, tensile testing
nodes.key_findings: Thiourea forms nonlinear zigzag H-bonded arrays (cis/trans + strained trans/trans) → amorphous despite dense H-bonds; urea forms only linear trans/trans → semicrystalline (UEG₃); TUEG₃: elastic modulus 1.4 GPa, stress at break 45±8 MPa, elongation 393±5%; complete healing at 24°C in 6 hours under 1 MPa compression; interfacial adhesion energy ~0.9 J/m²; flow transition relaxation time τ = 10⁵-10⁷ s (days to months) >> healing time (hours) → healing NOT from chain flow but from rapid H-bond exchange; structural element for activating H-bond pair exchange is key
nodes.scientific_questions: What activates H-bond exchange without chain diffusion? Can this principle extend to other H-bonding motifs? What determines the nonlinear geometry of thiourea arrays?

nodes.label: Low-Temperature Solid-State Polycondensation Synthesis of Non-Isocyanate Polyurethanes: A Pathway to
nodes.full_title: Low-Temperature Solid-State Polycondensation Synthesis of Non-Isocyanate Polyurethanes: A Pathway to High Molar Mass, Performance, and Reprocessability
nodes.authors: Dominik Wołosz, Aleksandra Marta Fage, Grzegorz Węgrzyk, Martyna Musiatowicz, Subrajeet Deshmukh, Maria Balk, Tomasz Gołofit, Robert Brüll
nodes.materials_raw: Non-isocyanate poly(carbonate-urethane)s (NIPCUs), biscarbamate (BC from p-xylylenediamine + ethylene carbonate), oligo(hexamethylene carbonate) diol (OCD, Mn=2000), titanium(IV) tert-butoxide catalyst
nodes.methods_raw: Solid-state polycondensation (SSP), melt polycondensation, FT-IR, NMR, SEC, DSC, TGA, WAXS, DMA, tensile testing
nodes.key_findings: SSP at 80-130°C (well below melt polycondensation at 180°C); SSP suppresses side reactions and thermal degradation; crystalline phase frozen during SSP → minimized side reactions; amorphous phase retains end-group mobility for reaction; SSP products have higher molar mass, toughness, and aesthetic quality than melt products; highly ordered crystalline phase formed; NIPCUs maintain performance over multiple reprocessing cycles; Tg ≈ 50°C for prepolymer; SSP window = Tg + 10 to Tm - 10-40°C
nodes.scientific_questions: What is the optimal particle size for SSP kinetics? How does crystallinity evolution during SSP affect final properties? Can SSP be applied to other transurethanization chemistries?

nodes.label: Vinylogous Urea - Urethane Vitrimers: Accelerating and Inhibiting Network Dynamics through Hydrogen
nodes.full_title: Vinylogous Urea - Urethane Vitrimers: Accelerating and Inhibiting Network Dynamics through Hydrogen Bonding
nodes.authors: Stéphanie Engelen, Neil D. Dolinski, Chuqiao Chen, Elina Ghimire, Charlie A. Lindberg, Alex E. Crolais, Natsumi Nitta, Johan M. Winne, Stuart J. Rowan, Filip E. Du Prez
nodes.materials_raw: Vinylogous urethane (VU_O, from acetoacetate), vinylogous urea (VUN, from acetoacetamide), poly(propylene glycol) trifunctional amine crosslinker
nodes.methods_raw: DSC, DMA (DTR, tanδ), WAXS, FTIR, model compound kinetics (¹H NMR), tensile testing
nodes.key_findings: In solution, VUN exchanges much faster than VU_O; in bulk networks, VU_O and VUN vitrimers show comparable stress relaxation → counterintuitive; VUN enables intermolecular H-bonding (bifurcated) vs VU_O intramolecular (pseudo-cyclic); at low VUN concentration, H-bonds catalyze covalent exchange (10× acceleration); at high VUN concentration, phase separation and H-bond network inhibit dynamics; bimodal tanδ for >50% VUN content; Tg increases from 7°C (VU_O100) to 34°C (VUN100); WAXS confirms phase separation at high VUN; same functional group can both accelerate and inhibit dynamics depending on concentration
nodes.scientific_questions: What is the critical VUN concentration for phase inversion? Can the catalytic H-bond effect be isolated from the inhibitory phase separation? How does this design principle extend to other dynamic covalent chemistries?

nodes.label: Tough and Stretchable All-κ-Carrageenan Hydrogel Based on the Cooperative Effects between Chain Conf
nodes.full_title: Tough and Stretchable All-κ-Carrageenan Hydrogel Based on the Cooperative Effects between Chain Conformation Transition and Stepwise Mechanical Training
nodes.authors: Qingye Liu, Jianfeng Zhang, Yarui Hou, Xiaohui Wang, Xiaojun Li, Tie Chen, Xiaojuan Xu
nodes.materials_raw: κ-carrageenan (κCG, Mη=4.20×10⁵ Da), NaOH/urea solvent, epichlorohydrin (ECH) crosslinker, KCl
nodes.methods_raw: Freeze-thawing dissolution, stepwise mechanical training, SEM, XRD, AFM, solid-state ¹³C NMR, SAXS, TGA, tensile testing
nodes.key_findings: NaOH/urea + freeze-thawing converts κCG from random coil to stiff chain conformation → homogeneous gel; stretchability increases from 42.1% to 156% with similar fracture stress; stepwise mechanical training + KCl incubation aligns helical segments → crystallinity and anisotropy increase; final gel: fracture strain 396%, stress ~0.55 MPa, toughness ~1.52 MJ/m³; 8.5× strain improvement, 11.5× toughness improvement vs traditional κCG gel; fast self-strengthening behavior; shape-memory and recovery under medium deformation; all-polysaccharide (no synthetic polymer)
nodes.scientific_questions: What is the mechanism of conformational stiffening in NaOH/urea? How does mechanical training quantitatively affect helical alignment? Long-term stability in physiological conditions?

nodes.label: Synthesis of Topological Gels by Penetrating Polymerization Using a Molecular Net
nodes.full_title: Synthesis of Topological Gels by Penetrating Polymerization Using a Molecular Net
nodes.authors: Yuichi Ohya, Ryota Dohi, Fumika Seko, Yuto Nakazawa, Ken-ichiro Mizuguchi, Kosei Shinzaki, Takahiko Yasui, Hiroaki Ogawa, Shizuka Kato, Yuta Yoshizaki, Nobuo Murase, Akinori Kuzuya
nodes.materials_raw: Molecular net (MN) from 4-armed PEG-NH₂ + 4-armed PEG-OSu (MW=10,000), N-isopropylacrylamide (NIPAAm), PNIPAAm
nodes.methods_raw: SEC with light scattering, DLS, SEM, AFM, swelling tests, tensile testing, cycle tests
nodes.key_findings: MN synthesized at 0.4 wt% PEG concentration → soluble product (vs gel at ≥0.5 wt%); MN MW = 1.90×10⁷ g/mol, Dz = 112 nm; "penetrating polymerization" of NIPAAm in presence of MN → topological gel; MN gel: no attractive interactions between components (ideal topological gel); swelling ratio ~6500% (vs ~2000% for chemically crosslinked gel); MN gel dissolves after 19 days (physical constraints only); cycle test shows increasing extensibility with each step; new paradigm in gel science
nodes.scientific_questions: Can MN gel stability be improved without covalent crosslinks? What is the mesh size distribution of MN? How does penetrating polymerization efficiency scale with MN concentration?

nodes.label: Moderated Ionic Bonding for Water-Free Recyclable Polyelectrolyte Complex Materials
nodes.full_title: Moderated Ionic Bonding for Water-Free Recyclable Polyelectrolyte Complex Materials
nodes.authors: Sophie G. M. van Lange, Diane W. te Brake, Giuseppe Portale, Anbazhagan Palanisamy, Joris Sprakel, Jasper van der Gucht
nodes.materials_raw: Polycation (vinylbenzyl chloride + quaternization with alkyl chains), polyanion (styrenesulfonyl imides + fluoroalkyl tails), ionic liquids (IL-S, IL), dioctylphthalate (DOP)
nodes.methods_raw: Hot-pressing, TGA (isothermal), DMA, tensile testing, WAXD, XPS, SAXS (static + temperature-resolved), SEM
nodes.key_findings: Hydrophobic tails on charged moieties moderate ionic bond strength intrinsically; NS (non-screened) = too strong, cannot process; HS (half-screened) = processable at 180°C but degrades on recycling; S (screened) = processable at 120°C, fully recyclable with no performance loss; 100% charge density but water-insensitive; insoluble in organic solvents; ionic liquid plasticizers can tune Tg (84°C with 20% IL-S, 140°C with 35% IL); "compleximers" = thermoplastics with thermoset characteristics; neutral plasticizers (DOP) cause phase separation → incompatible; ionic liquids integrate well due to chemical similarity
nodes.scientific_questions: What is the minimum tail length for sufficient screening? How does charge density affect the screening requirement? Can compleximers be used as coatings or adhesives?

nodes.label: Ultra-Stretchable and Fast Self-Healing Ionic Hydrogel in Cryogenic Environments for Artificial Nerv
nodes.full_title: Ultra-Stretchable and Fast Self-Healing Ionic Hydrogel in Cryogenic Environments for Artificial Nerve Fiber
nodes.authors: Chan Wang, Ying Liu, Xuecheng Qu, Bojing Shi, Qiang Zheng, Xubo Lin, Shengyu Chao, Changyong Wang, Jin Zhou, Yu Sun, Gengsheng Mao, Zhou Li
nodes.materials_raw: PVA + branched PEI (b-PEI) + LiCl (25 wt%) ternary system (SSIH)
nodes.methods_raw: DMA, ¹H NMR, XPS, MD simulation, tensile testing, impedance spectroscopy, LF-NMR
nodes.key_findings: LiCl strong hydration (ΔG° = -467 kJ/mol) breaks H-bond crystalline arrangement → anti-freezing; Tg = -71.55°C; stretchability 7046% (25% LiCl); conductivity 11.76 S/m; self-healing within 10 min at RT (84.31% efficiency); self-healing at -80°C (30 min, 63% efficiency); 92% water retention after 12 months; artificial nerve fiber demonstrates signal transmission at -80°C; comprehensive table comparing 34 anti-freezing self-healing hydrogels
nodes.scientific_questions: How does Li+ hydration shell structure change at cryogenic temperatures? What is the minimum LiCl concentration for anti-freezing? Can this system work with other salts?

nodes.label: Organogels versus Hydrogels: Advantages, Challenges, and Applications
nodes.full_title: Organogels versus Hydrogels: Advantages, Challenges, and Applications
nodes.authors: Mariia A. Kuzina, Dmitrii D. Kartsev, Alexander V. Stratonovich, Pavel A. Levkin
nodes.materials_raw: Various organogel systems (oleogels, ionogels, PEGgels, fluorogels, magnetogels, organohydrogels, bigels)
nodes.methods_raw: Review article covering classification, properties, and applications
nodes.key_findings: Organogels = gels with organic liquid phase (2253 papers 2012-2021 vs 70956 for hydrogels); key advantages: broader solvent choice, thermal stability (high boiling point solvents), environmental stability (lubricants for hydrophobic/ice-phobic/anti-biofouling), conductivity maintenance at extreme temperatures; binary solvent systems (water-DMSO to -140°C, water-EG to -40°C); multi-stimuli response; special wettability; organogels underrepresented but potentially superior to hydrogels in many applications
nodes.scientific_questions: Systematic structure-property relationships for organogel gelators? Scalable fabrication methods? Long-term stability under cycling?

nodes.label: Dissolution-Crystallization Transition within a Polymer Hydrogel for a Processable Ultratough Electr
nodes.full_title: Dissolution-Crystallization Transition within a Polymer Hydrogel for a Processable Ultratough Electrolyte
nodes.authors: Junjie Wei, Gumi Wei, Yinghui Shang, Jie Zhou, Chu Wu, Qigang Wang
nodes.materials_raw: Acrylamide (AAm, 15 wt%), NaAc·3H₂O crystals, DMAPS (zwitterionic monomer), agar, CMC, Na₂S₂O₃·5H₂O, MgSO₄·7H₂O
nodes.methods_raw: UV polymerization, XRD, SEM, compression/tensile testing, EIS (ionic conductivity), LF-NMR, molecular simulation (Gaussian), 3D printing
nodes.key_findings: NaAc crystallization within hydrogel → needle-like crystals (2-3 μm) forming parallel arrangement; compressive modulus increases 26000× (0.018→474.24 MPa at NaAc ratio 1.6); optimal NaAc ratio 1.0-1.4 for toughness (61.4 MPa at 95% strain); ionic conductivity 0.1-1.0 S/m (decreases with NaAc due to bound water); molecular simulation: PAM-NaAc·3H₂O interaction energy -27.27 kcal/mol vs PAM-H₂O -16.22 kcal/mol; dissolution-crystallization reversible by temperature; thermal-resistant: internal temperature only 50°C in fire (vs air 250°C); 3D printable; works -40°C to 80°C; universal for multiple hydrated salts (Na₂S₂O₃, MgSO₄)
nodes.scientific_questions: What controls crystal morphology and orientation? How does the dissolution-crystallization cycling affect long-term stability? Can this approach work with non-aqueous electrolytes?

nodes.label: A hyperelastic hydrogel with an ultralarge reversible biaxial strain
nodes.full_title: A hyperelastic hydrogel with an ultralarge reversible biaxial strain
nodes.authors: Lili Chen, Zhekai Jin, Wenwen Feng, Lin Sun, Hao Xu, Chao Wang
nodes.materials_raw: Poly[2-(acryloyloxy)ethyl]trimethylammonium chloride (PAETC, AETC-25 hydrogel, 25 wt% polymer)
nodes.methods_raw: AFM, cryo-TEM, in-situ SAXS, NMR, SLS, DSC, rheology, compression/tensile cycling
nodes.key_findings: Pearl-necklace chain (PNC) structure: sub-2 nm beads connected by hydrated strings from heterogeneous hydration of polyelectrolyte; maximum deformation: l_PNC between N^1/2 (FJC) and N^2/3 (CC); uniaxial stretchability 3000%; biaxial areal strain >10,000%; in-situ SAXS confirms reversible bead unfolding/refolding; reversibility U₂/U₁ = 98.9% at 1500% strain; 98%+ maintained over multiple cycles; compression: 1000 cycles at 67% strain with nearly overlapping curves; self-healing from needle punctures; no chemical crosslinkers needed; pneumatic gripper demonstration
nodes.scientific_questions: What determines bead size distribution? Can PNC concept extend to other polyelectrolytes? How does water content precisely control PNC formation?

nodes.label: Toughening Hydrogels with Small Molecules: Tiny Matter, Big Impact
nodes.full_title: Toughening Hydrogels with Small Molecules: Tiny Matter, Big Impact
nodes.authors: Zhihua Sha, Xin Chen, Hao Song, Yong Zheng, Wei Cui, Rong Ran
nodes.materials_raw: Review covering ions, solvents, monomers, biomass-derived small molecules in hydrogel toughening
nodes.methods_raw: Review article organizing toughening strategies by chemical nature of small molecules
nodes.key_findings: Small molecules induce structural transformations: ionic crosslinking, salting-out, phase separation, crystallization, mechanochemical network growth; unlike polymer-centric strategies (double network, nanofillers, strain-induced crystallization), small-molecule approaches are simpler and more scalable; Lake-Thomas theory explains baseline fracture energy (~10¹ nm dissipation zone); design principle: embed energy-dissipating mechanisms for bulk dissipation (Γ_bulk); applications in bioelectronics, soft robotics, water harvesting, adaptive adhesives
nodes.scientific_questions: Quantitative prediction of small molecule effects on fracture energy? Universal design rules across different small molecule types? Long-term stability of small molecule-mediated toughening?

nodes.label: Review article organizing toughening strategies by
Self-healable, Regenerable, and Degradable Dynamic Covalent Nitroalcohol Organogels
nodes.full_title: Self-healable, Regenerable, and Degradable Dynamic Covalent Nitroalcohol Organogels
nodes.authors: Yunchuan Qi, Mubarak Ayinla, Margaret J. Sobkowicz, Olof Ramström
nodes.materials_raw: 2,4,6-pyridinetricarboxaldehyde + α,ω-dinitroalkanes (2a-h), DMSO solvent, triethylamine catalyst
nodes.methods_raw: ¹H NMR monitoring, oscillatory rheology, FTIR, tube inversion gelation test, xerogel formation
nodes.key_findings: Nitroaldol (Henry) reaction forms dynamic C-C bonds in organogel network; gelation in ~825 s (G' crossover); G' = 6.6×10⁴ Pa; gelation tunable by concentration (>0.3 M needed), catalyst loading (5-25 mol% Et₃N, 35→7 min), temperature (25-60°C optimal, >65°C retro-reaction); even-carbon dinitroalkanes gel better than odd (odd-even alternation); xerogels reform gel upon DMSO addition; depolymerization with excess nitromethane; dual-entry equilibrium confirms reversibility
nodes.scientific_questions: What controls the odd-even gelation pattern? Can nitroaldol gels work in aqueous systems? Mechanical properties quantification?

nodes.label: Locking aggregation states in tough hydrogels through protective domain formation
nodes.full_title: Locking aggregation states in tough hydrogels through protective domain formation
nodes.authors: Hao Nan Qiu, Chuan Wei Zhang, Lixin Dai, Yichen Yan, Ping He, Ruoyi Ke, Xiaobing Zuo, Hua Zhou, Ximin He
nodes.materials_raw: PVA + KPS/TMEDA (free radical crosslinking system)
nodes.methods_raw: SAXS/WAXS (Beaucage model), DSC, FTIR, SEM, cyclic tensile testing, rheology, FEA simulation
nodes.key_findings: Sequential dehydration-induced crystallization + free radical crosslinking (FRC) creates protective domains around crystallites; protective domains = chemically-crosslinked loose aggregates surrounding crystallites; dual function: (1) shield physical crosslinks from water, (2) store hidden chain length for extensibility; PVA-FRC: E = 2.88 MPa, σ_b = 8.70 MPa, ε_b = 855%, toughness 37 MJ/m³; 2.4× modulus, 2.1× strain, 4.8× toughness vs unprotected; strength retention >80% after water immersion (vs ~20% for freeze-thawed); volume expansion <5% (vs 20-30% typical); resistant to salting-in ions (NaCl, NaBr, NaNO₃, MgCl₂, CaCl₂) and extreme pH (1-14); Mullins effect observed in single-network system; zinc-ion battery cycling >2343 h; generalizable to freeze-thawed, salting-out, cononsolvency PVA hydrogels
nodes.scientific_questions: What is the optimal FRC density for balancing protection and extensibility? Can protective domain concept apply to non-PVA systems? How does cycling affect protective domain integrity?

nodes.label: Triboelectric nanogenerator: from alternating current to direct current
nodes.full_title: Triboelectric nanogenerator: from alternating current to direct current
nodes.authors: Di Liu, Linglin Zhou, Zhong Lin Wang, Jie Wang
nodes.materials_raw: Review covering TENG materials (FEP, liquid metals, polymers)
nodes.methods_raw: Review of TENG physics, working modes, DC-TENG designs
nodes.key_findings: Four AC-TENG modes: contact-separation, lateral-sliding, single-electrode, freestanding; Maxwell's displacement current as fundamental driving force; electrostatic breakdown limits σ_AC-TENG = min(σ_triboelectrification, σ_r,air breakdown, σ_dielectric breakdown); DC-TENG via electrostatic breakdown, rotary rectifier, multiphase rotation; constant current TENG for IoT; figure of merit (FOM) framework: CFT > CS > SFT > LS > SEC
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving stable constant DC output? Maximizing charge density while avoiding breakdown? Scaling TENG for practical power generation?

nodes.label: Highly Robust and Environment-Tolerant Conducting Polymer Organohydrogels through the Synergy of Sal
nodes.full_title: Highly Robust and Environment-Tolerant Conducting Polymer Organohydrogels through the Synergy of Salting-Out Effect and In Situ Polymerization
nodes.authors: Na Li, Shide Liu, Xiaojiao Shi, Yuxuan Qiao, Ya Liang, Xiaoxiang Li, Zhihui Qin, Tifeng Jiao
nodes.materials_raw: PVA + pyrrole (Py) + Fe₂(SO₄)₃ + DMSO/H₂O binary solvent → PVA_DH/PPy-Fe⁻OHs
nodes.methods_raw: FTIR, XRD, Raman, SEM, TEM, tensile testing, panttear test, conductivity measurement
nodes.key_findings: Fe₂(SO₄)₃ serves dual function: (1) salting-out agent for PVA chain aggregation, (2) oxidant for in-situ Py polymerization to PPy; DMSO/H₂O binary solvent: cononsolvency effect + antifreezing + good medium for Py polymerization; crystallinity: 2.03% (PVA) → 10.30% (PVA/Py-OHs) → 18.18% (PVA/PPy-Fe-OHs); fracture stress 7.93 MPa, toughness 22.64 MJ/m³, stretchability 582%, conductivity 0.37 S/m; environmental stability -45 to 65°C; wearable sensor for human motion detection; SOIP strategy generalizable to gelatin and agarose
nodes.scientific_questions: How does PPy morphology affect conductivity? Can SOIP work with other CP monomers (aniline, EDOT)? Long-term cycling stability of sensor?

nodes.label: A conceptual review on polymer electrolytes and ion transport models
nodes.full_title: A conceptual review on polymer electrolytes and ion transport models
nodes.authors: Shujahadeen B. Aziz, Thompson J. Woo, M.F.Z. Kadir, Hameed M. Ahmed
nodes.materials_raw: PEO, PVA, PMMA, PCL, chitosan, PVP, PVC, PVDF as host polymers; LiClO₄, NaSCN, etc. as salts
nodes.methods_raw: EIS (complex impedance spectroscopy), dielectric spectroscopy, conductivity measurements
nodes.key_findings: Four PE types: dry SPE, plasticized PE, gel PE, composite PE; ion transport mainly in amorphous region; Arrhenius model for decoupled systems, VTF model for coupled systems; reformulated Arrhenius: σ_dc(T) = σ₀ exp(-E_a/(k_B T ε₀)); dielectric constant critical for cationic transport; EIS separates bulk and electrode effects; percolation threshold concept for amorphous conducting pathways
nodes.scientific_questions: Quantitative prediction of conductivity from dielectric constant? Role of ion pairs vs free ions? Optimal filler loading for composite PE?

nodes.label: Ionic liquid-based gels for biomedical applications
nodes.full_title: Ionic liquid-based gels for biomedical applications
nodes.authors: Yiru Gao, Wenxin Zhang, Linyu Li, Zejun Wang, Yang Shu, Jianhua Wang
nodes.materials_raw: Ionic liquids (imidazolium, choline, phosphonium), PILs, PVA, GelMA, chitosan, PEGDA, various monomers
nodes.methods_raw: Review of radical polymerization (thermochemical, photochemical, redox), physical crosslinking, RAFT
nodes.key_findings: Three types: PIL hydrogels (PILs continuous, water dispersed), ionic gels (non-PIL polymer continuous, ILs dispersed), IL gels (PILs continuous, ILs dispersed); three generations of ILs: (1) imidazolium/halides, (2) tunable with biocompatible cations, (3) degradable amino acid/choline-based; preparation: thermal initiation (APS, AIBN, BPO), photo initiation (LAP, 2-hydroxy-2-methylpropiophenone), redox (APS/TEMED); applications: antibacterial, antitumor, drug delivery, hemostasis; PVA-based ionic gels via freeze-thaw + IL swelling; choline amino acid hydrogels with double-network structure
nodes.scientific_questions: Biocompatibility and toxicity of IL-based gels in vivo? Long-term stability of IL-loaded gels? Controlled release mechanisms from IL gels?

nodes.label: Molecular charge states and contact charge exchange in polymers
nodes.full_title: Molecular charge states and contact charge exchange in polymers
nodes.authors: Thomas J. Fabish, Charles B. Duke
nodes.materials_raw: Poly(styrene) (PS), poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA), styrene/MMA copolymers
nodes.methods_raw: Contact charge exchange spectroscopy, film potential measurement, molecular-orbital analysis
nodes.key_findings: Reversible sign change of contact charge with sequential metal contacts; charge states attributed to intrinsic localized molecular-ion states; charge penetration depth ~2 μm boundary layer; donor/acceptor state distributions in polymers; copolymer spectra predictable from homopolymer distributions; Fermi level defined as energy dividing acceptor and donor states
nodes.scientific_questions: What determines the energy distribution breadth of molecular-ion states? How do intermolecular relaxations shift state energies by several eV?

nodes.label: Soft Actuators for Small-Scale Robotics
nodes.full_title: Soft Actuators for Small-Scale Robotics
nodes.authors: Lindsey Hines, Kirstin Petersen, Guo Zhan Lum, Metin Sitti
nodes.institution: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems
nodes.materials_raw: Review covering polymers, gels, liquid-crystal elastomers (LCEs), shape-memory polymers (SMPs), dielectric elastomers, ionic polymers, ferrogels, hydrogels, CNTs, paper
nodes.methods_raw: Review of actuation mechanisms (thermal, light, pressure, chemical, electric/magnetic)
nodes.key_findings: Force scaling: most soft actuators scale with L² (cross-section), magnetic with L³, surface tension with L; mammalian muscle benchmark: 20% strain, 100 kPa stress, 8 kJ/m³ work density, 50 W/kg power; LCEs for large deformations via nematic-isotropic transition; SMPs remember shapes via Tg/Tm transitions; key metrics: strain, stress, energy density, power density, bandwidth, cycle life
nodes.scientific_questions: How to achieve high force at small scales? Integration of sensing and actuation? Biocompatibility for in vivo applications?

nodes.label: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems
Artificial Muscles: Mechanisms, Applications, and Challenges
nodes.full_title: Artificial Muscles: Mechanisms, Applications, and Challenges
nodes.authors: Seyed M. Mirvakili, Ian W. Hunter
nodes.materials_raw: Review covering nylon fibers, CNT yarns, shape-memory alloys (NiTi), ionic polymer-metal composites (IPMCs), dielectric elastomers, conducting polymers, piezoelectrics, magnetostrictive materials, pneumatic actuators
nodes.methods_raw: Review of actuation mechanisms, performance metrics comparison
nodes.key_findings: Biological muscle: 20% strain, 0.35 MPa stress, 40 kJ/m³ work density, >10⁹ cycle life; highly oriented nylon fibers: anisotropic thermal expansion, up to 49% contractile strain in coiled actuators; coiled nylon actuators at ~$5/kg; torsional energy harvesting from waste heat; multidirectional bending via differential heating; performance metrics: output strain, stress, energy density, power density, catch-state, bandwidth, efficiency
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving catch-state (lock-up) in artificial muscles? Improving cycle life beyond biological muscle? Scaling laws for different actuator types?

nodes.label: Electronic skin based on flexible capacitor
nodes.full_title: Electronic skin based on flexible capacitor
nodes.authors: Yinghan Wang, Qinshu Chen, Yuchen Li, Sitong Lu, Zhe Liu, Diansheng Chen
nodes.materials_raw: Copper foil, polyimide, polyurethane, supercapacitor material (coke particle carbon 24% + acetylene black 6% + silica gel 70%), FDC2214 chip, STM32
nodes.methods_raw: Capacitive sensing (proximity + pressure), FDC2214 capacitance-to-digital converter, double-layer sandwich structure
nodes.key_findings: Double-layer structure separates proximity and pressure sensing; supercapacitor dielectric layer improves sensitivity (28.6 pF/N for 0-0.7N range); proximity sensing via human body induction capacitance; 10×10 cm optimal sensor size; minimum force resolution 0.04N; equipotential ring eliminates edge capacitance effect; recovery time <2s
nodes.scientific_questions: How to extend proximity sensing range? Integration with flexible electronics for wearable applications? Long-term stability of supercapacitor dielectric layer?

nodes.label: Ionic Gels and Their Applications in Stretchable Electronics
nodes.full_title: Ionic Gels and Their Applications in Stretchable Electronics
nodes.authors: Haifei Wang, Ziya Wang, Jian Yang, Chen Xu, Qi Zhang, Zhengchun Peng
nodes.materials_raw: Ionic gels (PAM hydrogels, PVDF, ionic liquids), Ag nanowires, CNTs, graphene, conductive polymers
nodes.methods_raw: Review of ionic gel preparation (chemical/physical crosslinking), EDL transistor fabrication, sensor integration
nodes.key_findings: Ionic gels = polymer networks + electrolyte solutions; EDL capacitance >1 μF/cm² at gel-electrode interface; ionic gels maintain conductivity under large strains (lower resistance change than electronic conductors); alginate/PAM hybrid hydrogels: 9000 J/m² fracture energy, >2000% stretch, notch-insensitive; applications: E-skin, EDL transistors, displays, energy storage, actuators; challenge: water evaporation limits aqueous ionic gel stability
nodes.scientific_questions: How to prevent water evaporation in hydrogel-based devices? Long-term cycling stability of EDL devices? Biocompatibility for implantable applications?

nodes.label: Application of Ionic Liquids to Energy Storage and Conversion Materials and Devices
nodes.full_title: Application of Ionic Liquids to Energy Storage and Conversion Materials and Devices
nodes.authors: Masayoshi Watanabe, Morgan L. Thomas, Shiguo Zhang, Kazuhide Ueno, Tomohiro Yasuda, Kaoru Dokko
nodes.materials_raw: Ionic liquids (imidazolium, pyrrolidinium, etc.), Li/Na salts, protic ILs, N-doped carbons from ILs
nodes.methods_raw: Review of battery electrolyte design, fuel cell membrane fabrication, carbon precursor synthesis
nodes.key_findings: Three types of IL battery electrolytes: (i) low-melting Li/Na salts, (ii) Li/Na salts in RTILs, (iii) solvate ILs [A(solvent)n]+; ILs meet safety demands (nonvolatility, thermal stability); Li-sulfur batteries: ILs suppress polysulfide shuttle; Li-oxygen batteries: ILs as stable electrolytes; protic ILs for anhydrous fuel cells; IL-derived N-doped carbons for electrocatalysts and battery electrodes
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving sufficient ionic conductivity at room temperature? Understanding SEI formation in IL electrolytes? Scalable synthesis of IL-derived functional carbons?

nodes.label: Soft, Super-Elastic, All-Polymer Piezoelectric Elastomer for Artificial Electronic Skin
nodes.full_title: Soft, Super-Elastic, All-Polymer Piezoelectric Elastomer for Artificial Electronic Skin
nodes.authors: Youjun Guan, Lingjie Tu, Kunyu Ren, Xinchang Kang, Yu Tian, Weicheng Deng, Peng Yu, Chengyun Ning, Rumin Fu, Guoxin Tan, Lei Zhou
nodes.materials_raw: PVDF + polyacrylonitrile (PAN) + NaSS + MBA (crosslinker) + DMSO
nodes.methods_raw: Radical polymerization + freeze-drying, FTIR, XRD, DSC, SEM, piezoelectric d33 testing, COMSOL simulation
nodes.key_findings: Dipole-dipole interaction between -CN (PAN) and -CF2 (PVDF) induces α→β phase transition without poling; d33 = 63 pC/N at 12.5 wt% PVDF; stretchability 211-259%; supercompressible (99% strain without cracking); superelastic (100% recovery); Young's modulus 0.224-0.334 MPa (skin-like); Voc = 253 mV; response time 15 ms; detects gestures, pulse, throat vibrations; β-phase content 80% via dipole-dipole interaction
nodes.scientific_questions: Optimizing PVDF content for balanced piezoelectric and mechanical properties? Long-term fatigue resistance? Integration with wireless data transmission?

nodes.label: Mastering Superior Performance Origins of Ionic Polyurethane/Silica Hybrids
nodes.full_title: Mastering Superior Performance Origins of Ionic Polyurethane/Silica Hybrids
nodes.authors: Jean-Emile Potaufeux, Jérémy Odent, Delphine Notta-Cuvier, Sophie Barrau, Chiara Magnani, Rémi Delille, Chunbo Zhang, Guoming Liu, Emmanuel P. Giannelis, Alejandro J. Muller, Franck Lauro, Jean-Marie Raquez
nodes.materials_raw: Imidazolium-functionalized PEG-based polyurethane (im-PU) + sulfonate silica nanoparticles (SiO2-SO3H)
nodes.methods_raw: DMTA, TTS rheology, DSC, TGA, tensile testing, STEM, dielectric spectroscopy
nodes.key_findings: Ionic imidazolium-sulfonate crosslinks create temporary crosslinks between polymer chains; strain-rate-dependent behavior (yield stress, modulus, ultimate strain increase with strain rate); characteristic time for ionic bond reformation quantified; dissociation dynamics of ionic crosslinks responsible for shape-memory and self-healing; nanoparticle reinforcement + ionic interactions synergistic
nodes.scientific_questions: Quantifying individual contributions of nanoparticles vs ionic interactions? Optimal ionic crosslink density? Temperature dependence of bond reformation kinetics?

nodes.label: Solvent-Free and Skin-Like Supramolecular Ion-Conductive Elastomers with Versatile Processability fo
nodes.full_title: Solvent-Free and Skin-Like Supramolecular Ion-Conductive Elastomers with Versatile Processability for Multifunctional Ionic Tattoos and on-Skin Bioelectronics
nodes.authors: Wenwen Niu, Qiong Tian, Zhiyuan Liu, Xiaokong Liu
nodes.materials_raw: IP6 (phytic acid) + PVA → SF-supra-ICE; PEDOT:PSS hydrogel; SEBS
nodes.methods_raw: Freeze-thaw cycles, FTIR, WAXD, DSC, TGA, impedance spectroscopy, electrophysiological measurements (ECG/EMG/EEG), in vivo biocompatibility
nodes.key_findings: Hydrogen-bonding between IP6 and PVA creates solvent-free ion-conductive elastomer; strain-induced crystallization at >500% strain; 32× stiffness enhancement via differential modulus; stable for 150 days in air (vs hydrogels dehydrate in hours); paintable/ printable "ionic tattoos" for ECG/EMG/EEG monitoring; excellent biocompatibility (oral gavage safety in mice); MVTR comparable to skin
nodes.scientific_questions: Mechanism of strain-induced crystallization in IP6-PVA? Long-term stability under mechanical cycling? Scaling to large-area bioelectronic applications?

nodes.label: A review of electrolyte materials and compositions for electrochemical supercapacitors
nodes.full_title: A review of electrolyte materials and compositions for electrochemical supercapacitors
nodes.authors: Cheng Zhong, Yida Deng, Wenbin Hu, Jinli Qiao, Lei Zhang, Jiujun Zhang
nodes.materials_raw: Aqueous electrolytes, organic electrolytes, ionic liquids, solid-state/quasi-solid-state electrolytes, redox-active electrolytes
nodes.methods_raw: Review of EDLC, pseudocapacitor, and hybrid supercapacitor electrolyte design
nodes.key_findings: Energy density E = ½CV²; increasing voltage more effective than capacitance for energy density; aqueous ES: ~1.0-1.3V window; organic ES: 2.5-2.7V; IL ES: 3.5-4.0V; requirements: wide potential window, high ionic conductivity, chemical/electrochemical stability, wide temperature range, low cost; matching electrolyte ion size with electrode pore size critical for performance
nodes.scientific_questions: Developing electrolytes with both wide voltage window and high conductivity? Understanding electrolyte-electrode interactions at molecular level? Solid-state electrolytes for flexible supercapacitors?

nodes.label: Ionic liquid gel materials: applications in green and sustainable chemistry
nodes.full_title: Ionic liquid gel materials: applications in green and sustainable chemistry
nodes.authors: Patricia C. Marr, Andrew C. Marr
nodes.materials_raw: Ionic liquids, polymer gelators, molecular gelators, colloidal particles, sol-gel materials
nodes.methods_raw: Review of gel classification (physical/chemical, rheological, solvent type, solid phase type), sol-gel process
nodes.key_findings: Gels classified by: (1) interactions (physical vs chemical), (2) rheology (G' plateau >> G''), (3) solid phase (colloidal, molecular, polymer); IL gels combine IL properties with gel structure; 10^18 possible IL combinations; applications: catalysis, energy, electronics, medicine; sol-gel process for inorganic-organic hybrids; aerogels/xerogels from solvent removal
nodes.scientific_questions: Designing task-specific IL gelators? Understanding gelation mechanisms in IL systems? Scalable manufacturing of IL gels?

nodes.label: Polymer electrolytes for lithium polymer batteries
nodes.full_title: Polymer electrolytes for lithium polymer batteries
nodes.authors: Lizhen Long, Shuanjin Wang, Min Xiao, Yuezhong Meng
nodes.materials_raw: PEO, polymer-in-salt (rubbery electrolytes), single-ion conducting polymers, gel polymer electrolytes (GPEs), inorganic fillers
nodes.methods_raw: Review of ion transfer mechanisms (VTF and Arrhenius models), electrolyte design strategies
nodes.key_findings: Two categories: solid polymer electrolytes (SPE) and gel polymer electrolytes (GPE); SPE conductivity <10⁻⁵ S/cm; GPE conductivity ~10⁻³ S/cm; VTF model for coupled ion-polymer motion; Arrhenius for decoupled hopping; PEO most studied matrix; Li⁺ coordination with ether oxygens; crystalline PEO₆-LiX can conduct via cylindrical tunnels; requirements: high conductivity, Li⁺ transference number ~1, mechanical strength, 4-5V electrochemical window
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving room-temperature conductivity >10⁻⁴ S/cm in SPEs? Suppressing dendrite growth in polymer electrolytes? Understanding ion transport in crystalline vs amorphous phases?

nodes.label: Towards flexible solid-state supercapacitors for smart and wearable electronics
nodes.full_title: Towards flexible solid-state supercapacitors for smart and wearable electronics
nodes.authors: Deepak P. Dubal, Nilesh R. Chodankar, Do-Heyoung Kim, Pedro Gomez-Romero
nodes.materials_raw: Carbon materials, conducting polymers, MXenes, metal nitrides, MOFs, black phosphorus, gel electrolytes (aqueous, organic, IL, redox-active)
nodes.methods_raw: Review of EDLC/pseudocapacitor mechanisms, flexible electrode design, gel electrolyte synthesis
nodes.key_findings: FSSCs use solid-state gel electrolyte + flexible electrodes; pore size ~2× electrolyte ion size optimal for EDLC; volumetric capacitance increases 1.5× when pore <1 nm; pseudocapacitance from underpotential deposition, redox, and intercalation mechanisms; b-value from log(i) vs log(ν) distinguishes diffusion-controlled (b=0.5) vs capacitive (b=1) processes; emerging materials: MXenes, metal nitrides, MOFs, POMs, black phosphorus
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving battery-level energy density while maintaining supercapacitor power density? Flexible device integration with energy harvesters? Long-term mechanical cycling stability?

nodes.label: Electrolyte selection for supercapacitive devices: a critical review
nodes.full_title: Electrolyte selection for supercapacitive devices: a critical review
nodes.authors: Bhupender Pal, Shengyuan Yang, Subramaniam Ramesh, Venkataraman Thangadurai, Rajan Jose
nodes.materials_raw: Aqueous, organic, ionic liquid, solid-state, and redox electrolytes for supercapacitors
nodes.methods_raw: Review of electrode-electrolyte interaction, conductivity mechanisms, electrochemical stability
nodes.key_findings: σ = Σnᵢmᵢzᵢe; conductivity depends on solvation, migration, lattice energy; binary solvent mixtures (e.g., EC+DMC) higher conductivity than single solvents; salt concentration optimization balances free ions vs viscosity; "water in salt" electrolytes: non-flammable, wide voltage window; electrochemical stability depends on solvent, salt type, and electrode; thermal stability analyzed by TGA/DSC
nodes.scientific_questions: Universal electrolyte design principles for different SCD types? Understanding ion solvation effects on capacitance? Developing electrolytes for extreme temperatures?

nodes.label: Stretchable and tough conductive hydrogels for flexible pressure and strain sensors
nodes.full_title: Stretchable and tough conductive hydrogels for flexible pressure and strain sensors
nodes.authors: Zhenwu Wang, Yang Cong, Jun Fu
nodes.materials_raw: Conductive hydrogels (electron-conductive: carbon/metal fillers, PEDOT; ion-conductive: polyelectrolyte, salt-loaded), DN hydrogels, nanocomposite hydrogels
nodes.methods_raw: Review of tough hydrogel strategies (DN, nanocomposite, micelle-crosslinked, polyampholyte), sensor fabrication
nodes.key_findings: DN hydrogels: first network dissipates energy, second provides elasticity; alginate/PAAm: 9000 J/m² fracture energy, 2000% stretch; nanocomposite hydrogels using clay nanosheets (LAPONITE); PEDOT interpenetrating DN hydrogels: 470 MPa compressive strength; ion-conductive hydrogels more biomimetic than electron-conductive; sensor types: resistance and capacitance; tissue adhesion important for implantable sensors
nodes.scientific_questions: Balancing conductivity with mechanical toughness? Long-term stability of conductive networks under cyclic loading? Biocompatibility of conductive fillers for in vivo applications?

nodes.label: Electrolytes in Dye-Sensitized Solar Cells
nodes.full_title: Electrolytes in Dye-Sensitized Solar Cells
nodes.authors: Jihuai Wu, Zhang Lan, Jianming Lin, Miaoliang Huang, Yunfang Huang, Leqing Fan, Genggeng Luo
nodes.materials_raw: Liquid electrolytes (organic solvents, ionic liquids, I⁻/I₃⁻ redox couples), quasi-solid-state electrolytes (thermoplastic/thermosetting polymers, composites), solid-state transport materials (inorganic conductors, organic hole-transport materials, perovskites)
nodes.methods_raw: Review of DSSC electrolyte design, transport mechanisms, cell performance optimization
nodes.key_findings: DSSC structure: photoanode/dye/electrolyte/counter electrode; PCE = JscVocFF/Pin; photovoltage = difference between TiO₂ Fermi level and electrolyte redox potential; liquid electrolytes: highest efficiency but stability issues; quasi-solid-state: polymer gel electrolytes balance efficiency and stability; solid-state: spiro-OMeTAD as hole conductor; perovskite solar cells emerging with >19% PCE; ionic liquids as non-volatile electrolyte solvents
nodes.scientific_questions: Achieving long-term stability without sacrificing efficiency? Understanding charge recombination mechanisms? Developing efficient solid-state DSSCs?

nodes.label: Highly stretchable, tough, healable and mechanoresponsive polyurethane elastomers for flexible capac
nodes.full_title: Highly stretchable, tough, healable and mechanoresponsive polyurethane elastomers for flexible capacitor applications
nodes.authors: Amir Khan, Chuan-Fu Wang, Ravinder Reddy Kisannagar, Wei-Tsung Chuang, Pham Quoc Nhien, Sadiq Mahmood, Monica Katiyar, Dipti Gupta, Kung-Hwa Wei, Hong-Cheu Lin
nodes.institution: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University
nodes.materials_raw: Polyurethane + rhodamine mechanophore + Diels-Alder adduct + HEDS (disulfide) + PEG + HDI + TEA
nodes.methods_raw: Condensation polymerization, FTIR, Raman, SAXS, TGA, DSC, rheology, tensile testing, fluorescence spectroscopy
nodes.key_findings: 5500% stretchability, 324 MJ/m³ toughness; dual healing via disulfide exchange (60°C) and DA/retro-DA reactions (80-140°C); 87% healing efficiency after 24h; mechanochromic response: green (450-500 nm) → orange-red (588 nm) emission under strain via rhodamine close→open form transition; 90.9% shape recovery; dielectric permittivity 160 at 1 Hz; capacitance 0.28 nF/cm²; survived 1600 kg car compression
nodes.scientific_questions: Optimizing DA vs disulfide ratio for balanced healing speed and mechanical properties? Long-term cycling stability of mechanochromic response? Integration with electronic devices?

nodes.label: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University
Gel-Based Ionic Circuits
nodes.full_title: Gel-Based Ionic Circuits
nodes.authors: Hyunjae Yoo, Yun Hyeok Lee, Min-Gyu Lee, Jeong-Yun Sun
nodes.materials_raw: Hydrogels, organogels, ionogels, polyelectrolyte gels, zwitterionic gels, ionic liquids (EMIM-TFSI, EMIM-DCA), PAAm/LiCl hydrogels, PVA hydrogels, fluoroelastomer/IL composites, PDMS-based ionic conductors
nodes.methods_raw: Review of gel-based ionic circuit elements (resistors, capacitors, memristors, diodes, transistors), ionic conduction mechanisms, EDL formation, impedance spectroscopy
nodes.key_findings: Ionic circuits use ions (Na⁺, K⁺, Ca²⁺) as charge carriers instead of electrons; gels serve as self-standing solid-state ionic conductors; ionic conductivity σ = FΣ|zᵢ|uᵢcᵢ; polyelectrolyte gels enable ion-selective conduction (p-type/n-type) analogous to semiconductor diodes; zwitterionic gels have internal ion pairs enabling efficient transport; EDL capacitance orders of magnitude higher than conventional capacitors; ionic memristors emulate synaptic plasticity (STP, LTP, LTD, PPF, STDP); gel-based TENGs, thermoelectric generators, piezoionic generators; applications in touch panels (1000% areal strain), strain sensors, artificial synapses, neuromorphic computing, electro-osmosis, electrochromic devices, iontophoresis
nodes.scientific_questions: Bridging biological-artificial ionic systems? Achieving stable long-term ionic memory? Scaling ionic circuits for complex computation?

nodes.label: Review of gel-based ionic circuit elements (resist
Robust Ionic Gel Elastomers Derived from Molecularly Entangled Nodes
nodes.full_title: Robust Ionic Gel Elastomers Derived from Molecularly Entangled Nodes
nodes.authors: Honggang Mei, Chen Liu, Nan Jiang, Jiao Wang, Zejian He, Xue Yang, Yanfang Wang, Dong Zhao, Yuping Wang, Sheng Zhang, Guangfeng Li, Feihe Huang
nodes.materials_raw: Polyurethane (PU) + poly(ionic liquid) (PIL) + EMIM-TFSI + PTMG soft segment + HDI + Cu(I)-phenanthroline entangled nodes
nodes.methods_raw: FTIR, DMA, DSC, rheology (temperature sweep, stress relaxation), uniaxial tensile testing, EIS, cyclic stability testing
nodes.key_findings: Entangled ionic gel (EIG) elastomers: tensile strength 33.5 MPa, strain 4000%, toughness 509.7 MJ/m³; entangled nodes (Cu-phenanthroline coordination) enable stress-induced dissociation → chain slippage → energy dissipation while preserving network integrity; ionic conductivity 0.040 mS/cm; stable resistance monitoring over 7000 cycles at 100% strain; notch detection via resistance change; surpasses NR, TPU, silicone rubber in toughness; 0.17 g sample supports 5 kg dumbbell (30000× its weight)
nodes.scientific_questions: Optimizing entangled node density for balanced strength/conductivity? Extending to other polymer systems?

nodes.label: Ionic Liquid-Based Gels for Applications in Electrochemical Energy Storage and Conversion Devices: A
nodes.full_title: Ionic Liquid-Based Gels for Applications in Electrochemical Energy Storage and Conversion Devices: A Review of Recent Progress and Future Prospects
nodes.authors: Sharmin Sultana, Kumkum Ahmed, Prastika Krisma Jiwanti, Brasstira Yuva Wardhana, MD Nahin Islam Shiblee
